news.admin module

news.apps module

class news.apps.NewsConfig(app_name, app_module)[source]

Bases: AppConfig

name = 'news'
ready()[source]

When the app is ready, groups and permissions are set up for the project.

news.forgot_password module

news.forgot_password.build_email(user, reset_url)[source]
Parameters:

user – The user object for whom the password reset email is being

built. :param reset_url: The URL that the user will use to reset their password.

Build and return an email message for password reset.

Returns:

An EmailMessage object containing the password reset email.

news.forgot_password.change_user_password(username, password)[source]
Parameters:

  • username – The username of the user whose password is to be changed.

  • password – The new password to set for the user.

Saves the new password for the specified user.

news.forgot_password.generate_reset_url(user)[source]
Parameters:

user – The user who is requesting a password reset.

Returns:

A URL that the user can use to reset their password.

Generate a password reset URL for a given user. This function will create a unique token with an expiry time and returns a URL that the user can use to reset their password.

news.models module

This module defines the data models for the news application, including publishers, articles, newsletters, subscriptions, and password reset tokens.

class news.models.Article(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Model representing an article, indicating if it has been approved by an editor.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

author

Accessor to the related object on the forward side of a many-to-one or one-to-one (via ForwardOneToOneDescriptor subclass) relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Child.parent is a ForwardManyToOneDescriptor instance.

author_id
content

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

created_at

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

get_next_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=True, **kwargs)
get_previous_by_created_at(*, field=<django.db.models.fields.DateTimeField: created_at>, is_next=False, **kwargs)

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

class news.models.Publisher(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Model representing a publisher, which can have multiple editors and journalists.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

article_set

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

editors

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

id

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

journalists

Accessor to the related objects manager on the forward and reverse sides of a many-to-many relation.

In the example:

class Pizza(Model):
    toppings = ManyToManyField(Topping, related_name='pizzas')

Pizza.toppings and Topping.pizzas are ManyToManyDescriptor instances.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_forward_many_to_many_manager() defined below.

name

A wrapper for a deferred-loading field. When the value is read from this object the first time, the query is executed.

objects = <django.db.models.manager.Manager object>
subscription_set

Accessor to the related objects manager on the reverse side of a many-to-one relation.

In the example:

class Child(Model):
    parent = ForeignKey(Parent, related_name='children')

Parent.children is a ReverseManyToOneDescriptor instance.

Most of the implementation is delegated to a dynamically defined manager class built by create_reverse_many_to_one_manager() defined below.

class news.models.ResetToken(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: Model

Model representing a password reset token, which is associated with a user and has a token value, expiry date, and determines if it has been used.

exception DoesNotExist

Bases: ObjectDoesNotExist

exception MultipleObjectsReturned

Bases: MultipleObjectsReturned

exception NotUpdated

Bases: ObjectNotUpdated, DatabaseError

news.serializers module

class news.serializers.ArticleSerializer(*args, **kwargs)[source]

Bases: ModelSerializer

Serializer for the Article model, including nested serializers for author and publisher.

class Meta[source]

Bases: object

” Meta class to specify the model and fields for the ArticleSerializer

fields = ['id', 'title', 'content', 'author', 'publisher', 'created_at', 'is_approved']
model

alias of Article

Serializer for the Publisher model, id and name fields.

class Meta[source]

fields = ['id', 'name']
model

alias of Publisher

Serializer for the Subscription model, including serializers for reader, subscribed publisher and journalist.

Bases: object

Meta class to specify the model and fields for the SubscriptionSerializer.

fields = ['id', 'reader', 'subscribed_publisher', 'subscribed_journalist']

alias of Subscription

news.setup_groups module

news.setup_groups.create_project_groups(sender, **kwargs)[source]
Parameters:

  • sender – The model class that sent the signal.

  • kwargs – Additional keyword arguments.

Create ‘editor’ and ‘journalist’ groups if they don’t exist, and assign permissions to the ‘editor’ and ‘journalist’ groups.

news.tests module

class news.tests.SubscriptionAPITests(methodName='runTest')[source]

Bases: APITestCase

setUp()[source]

Set up the data for testing.

test_get_subscribed_articles_authenticated(mock_tweet)[source]

Verify the API returns only subscribed articles using Basic Auth.

test_get_subscribed_articles_unauthenticated()[source]

Verify that unauthenticated users are blocked.

news.tweet module

class news.tweet.Tweet[source]

Bases: object

authenticate()[source]
make_tweet(tweet_data)[source]

news.urls module

news.views module

This module contains the view logic for the news application, handling article displays, user authentication, and dashboards.

news.views.api_get_all_publishers(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Fetch all publishers registered in the system.

news.views.api_get_journalist_articles(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Retrieve all approved articles for a specific journalist.

news.views.api_get_publisher_articles(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Retrieve all approved articles for a specific publisher.

news.views.api_get_subscribed_articles(request, *args, **kwargs)[source]

Retrieve articles from publishers and journalists that the authenticated user has subscribed to.

news.views.approve_article(request, article_id)[source]

Approves an article if the editor belongs to the article’s publisher OR if the article is independent.

news.views.article_detail(request, article_id)[source]

Displays article details and allows readers to subscribe to the author or publisher.

news.views.article_list(request)[source]

Displays all articles that have been approved by an editor. This matches your ‘LATEST HEADLINES’ template logic.

news.views.delete_article(request, article_id)[source]

Deletes an article if the user is the author or an editor for the article’s publisher.

news.views.delete_newsletter(request, newsletter_id)[source]

Deletes a newsletter if the user is the author or an editor from the same publisher group.

news.views.edit_article(request, article_id)[source]
news.views.edit_newsletter(request, newsletter_id)[source]

” Allows the author of the newsletter or an editor from the same publisher group to edit the newsletter content.

news.views.editor_dashboard(request)[source]

Lists only articles the editor is authorized to approve.

news.views.is_editor(user)[source]
news.views.journalist_dashboard(request)[source]

Dashboard for Journalists to view their own articles and submit new ones for approval.

news.views.login_user(request)[source]

Handles user login using username and password. If successful, the user is redirected to the article list page. If the credentials are incorrect, an error is displayed.

news.views.logout_user(request)[source]

Logs out the current user and redirects to the login page.

news.views.my_subscriptions(request)[source]

Displays the user’s current subscriptions and allows them to unsubscribe.

news.views.newsletter_list(request)[source]

Editors see all newsletters. Journalists see only theirs.

news.views.register(request)[source]

Handles two-step registration: Step 1: Basic info + Role selection. Step 2: Publisher affiliation (for Journalists/Editors only).

news.views.resend_newsletter(request, newsletter_id)[source]

Resends a previously created newsletter to its subscribers.

news.views.reset_password_submit(request)[source]

Handles submit of new password, by validating token again and then changing the user’s password. It also marks the token as used and clears the session.

news.views.reset_user_password(request, token)[source]

Handles the password reset process by checking token is valid and not expired, then allows the user to set a new password.

news.views.send_newsletter(request)[source]

Creates a newsletter record in the database and sends it to subscribers.

news.views.send_password_reset(request)[source]

Handles the password reset request by generating a reset URL and sending a password reset email to the user. If email does not exist, it redirects to the login page without revealing that the email is not registered for security reasons.

Module contents
